Daily Ledger
World Business Science

Coastal cities prepare new flood defenses after record spring tides

Engineers and local officials say the latest models changed the timetable for several waterfront neighborhoods.

By Mira Chen, May 11, 2026

Live update: emergency managers will publish a revised shelter map before the weekend.

The city council approved a fast-track review of drainage tunnels, temporary sea walls, and neighborhood evacuation routes after a week of unusually high tides.

Residents near the old ferry terminal said water reached storefront entrances twice this month, disrupting deliveries and public transportation during the morning commute.

The most important thing now is predictable communication, not another dashboard that nobody checks during a storm.

Markets opened lower after chip exporters warned of delays.
